vulnerability.title: Ensure PAM Enforces Password Requirements - Minimum Uppercase Characters
vulnerability.description: The pam_pwquality module's ucredit= parameter controls requirements for usage of uppercase letters in a password. When set to a negative number, any password will be required to contain that many uppercase characters. When set to a positive number, pam_pwquality will grant +1 additional length credit for each uppercase character. Modify the ucredit setting in /etc/security/pwquality.conf to require the use of an uppercase character in passwords.
vulnerability.recommendation: Remediation script: # Remediation is applicable only in certain platforms if rpm --quiet -q kernel-core && { rpm --quiet -q libpwquality; }; then var_password_pam_ucredit='' if grep -sq ucredit /etc/security/pwquality.conf.d/*.conf ; then sed -i "/ucredit/d" /etc/security/pwquality.conf.d/*.conf fi # Strip any search characters in the key arg so that the key can be replaced without # adding any search characters to the config file. stripped_key=$(sed 's/[\^=\$,;+]*//g' <<< "^ucredit") # shellcheck disable=SC2059 printf -v formatted_output "%s = %s" "$stripped_key" "$var_password_pam_ucredit" # If the key exists, change it. Otherwise, add it to the config_file. # We search for the key string followed by a word boundary (matched by \>), # so if we search for 'setting', 'setting2' won't match. if LC_ALL=C grep -

triage_decision.reason: Adjusting the ucredit parameter in /etc/security/pwquality.conf enforces a password complexity requirement for uppercase characters. This is a standard PAM password policy change that does not affect active sessions, authentication mechanisms, or system services, and only impacts password changes at the next rotation.
triage_decision.estimated_impact: authentication/authorization affected; Existing passwords lacking an uppercase character will be rejected at the next password change, potentially causing user inconvenience; Misconfiguration could inadvertently disable the requirement if the value is set incorrectly

review_feedback: The plan uses write_file which may overwrite the entire /etc/security/pwquality.conf file, potentially removing other important pwquality settings and weakening password policy.

previous_review_verdicts.feedback: The plan correctly sets ucredit = -1 to require at least one uppercase character, which addresses the vulnerability. However, it could be improved for idempotency and safety.
concerns: Using write_file may overwrite the entire pwquality.conf file, potentially removing other important settings.
suggested_improvements: Backup the original file before modification.
Use a tool like augeas, sed, or lineinplace to ensure only the ucredit line is changed and other settings remain intact.
Verify the syntax after change (e.g., run pwqualitycheck or test password policy).
